hypothesis: A physics-informed foundation model that couples SMILES-based embeddings with differentiable multi-scale battery transport priors (molecular solvation energies, ionic diffusion coefficients, and interfacial energy penalties) will improve out-of-distribution generalization of electrolyte formulation-to-device predictions over a purely data-driven SMILESFM, evidenced by lower MAE and better predictive calibration on unseen chemistries and temperature regimes.
